scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a harvest-lot safety summary tied to field, water, sanitation, cooling, and traceability evidence without implying final release authority.
Incomplete field, animal-intrusion, corrective-action, and water evidence kept the lot on hold.
Repeat sanitation-control failures triggered corrective-action escalation rather than unsupported closure.
Harvest-window pressure did not bypass documented water corrective-action review.
Seasonal runoff and upstream livestock conditions were treated as workflow-changing dependencies requiring current evidence and escalation.
Conflicting lot and KDE records blocked release and required traceability reconciliation.
The lane refused agronomic, pesticide-label, and legal signoff while routing the food-safety evidence question.
Incomplete livestock treatment and withdrawal records triggered veterinary and final-disposition escalation.
Missing tenant facts and source-system context produced a blocked low-confidence response.
Conflicting tenant and buyer policy was escalated instead of converted into confident release support.
Environmental and grant signoff were rejected and routed to adjacent owners while preserving the food-safety evidence gap summary.
Tenant terminology was preserved while public-baseline assumptions and traceability applicability limits were recorded.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against a strict 0.92 threshold for agriculture food-safety evidence handling, hold and release boundaries, seasonal dependency handling, traceability discipline, and authority-sensitive escalation.
Functionality coverage is complete, including all shared abilities, all quality-safety-and-assurance abilities, and all agriculture-and-natural-resources abilities.
The package remains explicitly validated as a spec pack and does not claim retained-implementation runtime authority.
